Supabase vs Bubble: Which Is Better?

Choosing between Supabase and Bubble? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose Supabase if you need solo-devs or startups. Choose Bubble if you prioritize non-developers or startups.

Supabase

PricingFree / $25/mo Pro / $599/mo Team
Rating★★★★½ 4.5/5

Key Features

  • PostgreSQL database with SQL editor and table views
  • Authentication with email, OAuth, magic links, and multi-factor auth
  • Storage for files with S3-compatible API and CDN
  • Real-time subscriptions via WebSocket for live data syncing
  • Edge Functions for serverless compute at the edge
  • Auto-generated TypeScript SDK from your database schema

Pros

  • Free tier is genuinely generous — 2 projects, 500MB database, 50K monthly active users, 5GB bandwidth
  • PostgreSQL is a proper database, not a NoSQL toy; you get real SQL joins, indexes, and advanced queries
  • Auth is built-in and supports email/password, OAuth (Google, GitHub, etc.), and magic links out of the box
  • Real-time subscriptions are surprisingly easy to set up and work well for collaborative features
  • Open-source with a fair license means no vendor lock-in — you can self-host if needed

Cons

  • Scaling costs can be unpredictable; the jump from Free ($0) to Pro ($25) is fine, but Team ($599) is steep
  • Still maturing — some features (like Edge Functions) feel less polished than AWS Lambda or Vercel's offerings
  • Row Level Security (RLS) is powerful but complex; the learning curve for secure app setup is real
  • Database performance at scale requires PostgreSQL tuning knowledge that many developers don't have
  • The dashboard and UI, while improving, occasionally has reliability issues and slow loads

Bubble

PricingFree / $29/mo Starter / $119/mo Growth
Rating★★★★ 4.1/5

Key Features

  • Visual web application builder with drag-and-drop interface
  • Built-in database with user management, privacy rules, and data relationships
  • Workflow automation engine for business logic, conditional flows, and API calls
  • Responsive design engine for mobile-friendly applications
  • Plugin marketplace for Stripe, SendGrid, OpenAI, and 1,000+ integrations
  • Version control with branch testing and deployment management

Pros

  • You can build real, functional web apps without writing a single line of code — databases, workflows, responsive UIs, APIs
  • The visual workflow builder is powerful; conditional logic, data transforms, and API integrations are all drag-and-drop
  • Plugin ecosystem adds pre-built functionality for payments (Stripe), email (SendGrid), charts, maps, and more
  • Responsive engine lets you build mobile-friendly apps without separate mobile development
  • The Bubble community is active and supportive; forums, templates, and courses are widely available

Cons

  • Platform lock-in is real — migrating off Bubble means rebuilding your app from scratch
  • Performance limitations become apparent with complex apps; page load times and workflow execution speeds degrade
  • Cost scales with usage; the Growth plan ($119/mo) is where serious apps need to start
  • SEO for Bubble-built apps is poor compared to traditional web frameworks
  • Debugging in Bubble's visual editor is harder than reading code — tracing data flow issues is painful
Try Bubble →

Which Should You Choose?

Both Supabase and Bubble are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose Supabase if: Supabase is the open-source Firebase alternative that gives you a PostgreSQL database, authentication, storage, and real-time subscriptions in one package. It's popular with solo developers and startups who want the convenience of a backend-as-a-service without the proprietary lock-in. The generous free tier doesn't hurt either.
  • Choose Bubble if: Bubble is the most powerful no-code platform for building real web applications. We're not talking about landing pages or simple forms — people have built marketplaces, social networks, and SaaS products entirely in Bubble without writing code. The trade-off for that power is complexity; Bubble's learning curve is steeper than most no-code tools, and performance at scale can be an issue.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
